title: Add SQLite job to CI
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Adds a job to test Podman for the new SQLite database backend (or, more accurately, adds it to the jobs matrix). This will add it in some places where it isn't wired in yet (system tests) and potentially others where it's not desired (don't need to build Podman any different if sqlite is in use, but I think we will with this patch), but it seemed like the simplest way to get it in testing.

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: 16 root failures, 10 rootless. Rootless is probably a subset of root?
Seems like it's mostly simple stuff - we need to detect and handle name conflicts during volume and container creation, which should fix a bunch.
@Luap99 You OK with removing `Podman pod create [It] podman create pod with same name as ctr` without fixing it in this PR? I'd prefer to do the Bolt rewrite work all at once in another PR.

> @Luap99 You OK with removing `Podman pod create [It] podman create pod with same name as ctr` without fixing it in this PR? I'd prefer to do the Bolt rewrite work all at once in another PR.

You can skip the test and create an issue for it?  Just to be extra sure we're not forgetting it.

> > @Luap99 You OK with removing `Podman pod create [It] podman create pod with same name as ctr` without fixing it in this PR? I'd prefer to do the Bolt rewrite work all at once in another PR.
> 
> You can skip the test and create an issue for it? Just to be extra sure we're not forgetting it.

As long as it will be addressed before the next release I am fine with it, an issue to keep track of it sounds good.

Things still broken:
- Transient containers. Need to figure out how this actually works before I can identify what's going on.
- Image-backed volumes. Probably something simple, but I haven't managed to ID what yet.
Image volumes should be fixed. Just leaves transient containers.
Should be down to one failing test after this last push

In theory the test failures are fixed now.

Still to-do: Address Chris's comments RE: Cirrus configs, and get system tests wired up (will probably need help from @edsantiago for that). I'm going to pencil both of those in as "next week" things.
4 failures all looked like network flakes, restarted them all. Stripping WIP.

This doesn't look like a network flake:
```
[+5308s] Error: unknown flag: --database-backend
```
@mheon you've got a merge conflict with #17729; dunno if you want to fix & repush now, or wait-see if your latest fix passes CI.
For anyone interested in an eye exam: ![cirrus-map-pr17688](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/225441311-fb2e0472-28e3-4081-94a6-e4ef2a81e311.png)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ya, nice job Matt.  Lol Ed, ya pretty soon the tool's going to need renaming to `cirrus-maze-gen.sh`
